html:
[210]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 公无力备花烛，代以二油灯 - 公 is a respectful title for a man, possibly a scholar or official. 无力备花烛 means unable to prepare the wedding candles, referring to the lack of resources for a traditional wedding ceremony. 代以二油灯 means substituted with two oil lamps, indicating a simpler, more modest celebration.
  • 妾每见取妇家，必彩舆、鼓乐、鸣炮，君今一事不备，岂非妾命累君耶 - 妾 is a humble term for the wife speaking to her husband. 取妇家 refers to the wedding party of the bride's family. 彩舆、鼓乐、鸣炮 describe the traditional wedding procession, including decorated sedan chairs, music, and firecrackers. 君今一事不备 means you have not prepared any of these things, and 岂非妾命累君耶 is a rhetorical question meaning isn't it because of my fate that you are burdened?
  • 卿今羡此不得，恐异日闻之生厌耳 - 卿 is a term of endearment for the wife. 羡此不得 means you cannot envy this, implying that the wife cannot have the lavish wedding she desires. 恐异日闻之生厌耳 means I fear that one day you will grow tired of hearing about it, suggesting that the husband is concerned about the wife's future dissatisfaction.
  • 妾乐此不疲，何厌之有 - 乐此不疲 means enjoy it without tiring, and 何厌之有 is a rhetorical question meaning why would there be any weariness?
  • 未几，公入泮，将应秋闱，无力买舟，惟趁夜航之便，行百里，不过数十钱 - 未几 means soon after, and 入泮 refers to passing the local examination and entering the school. 将应秋闱 means about to take the autumn examination. 无力买舟 means unable to afford a boat, and 惟趁夜航之便 means only by taking advantage of the night ferry. 行百里，不过数十钱 means traveling a hundred miles, costing only a few dozen coins.
  • 无卧具者，例不准趁船 - 无卧具者 means those without bedding, and 例不准趁船 means by rule, not allowed to board the ship.
  • 盍以敝毡裹两破蒲团，用充卧具，谁敢拒之 - 盍 is a suggestion meaning why don't we. 敝毡 means old felt, and 两破蒲团 means two broken zafus (cushions). 用充卧具 means to serve as bedding, and 谁敢拒之 means who would dare refuse?
  • 公如法，趁船赴省 - 公如法 means the man followed the suggestion, and 趁船赴省 means boarded the ship to go to the provincial capital.
  • 停泊时，舟子举其具掷岸上树间 - 停泊时 means when the ship was moored, and 舟子举其具掷岸上树间 means the boatman threw the bedding onto a tree on the shore.
  • 公亦不顾，入茶肆，旋有驿卒乘骑至，亦系马树下 - 公亦不顾 means the man paid no attention, and 入茶肆 means went into a teahouse. 旋有驿卒乘骑至 means soon after, a postal official arrived on horseback, and 亦系马树下 means also tied his horse to a tree.
  • 马饥，见破毡内有草，蹄而食之 - 马饥 means the horse was hungry, and 见破毡内有草 means saw grass inside the old felt. 蹄而食之 means trotted up and ate it.
  • 群哗曰：「许爷行李被马吃矣。」公亦大笑而去 - 群哗曰 means the crowd shouted, and 许爷行李被马吃矣 means the official's luggage has been eaten by the horse. 公亦大笑而去 means the man also laughed and left.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 花烛 - Refers to the traditional wedding ceremony where candles are lit to symbolize the union of the couple.
  • 泮 - Refers to the local examination, which was a crucial step in the imperial examination system.
  • 秋闱 - Refers to the provincial examination, another important stage in the imperial examination system.
  • 夜航 - Refers to night ferries, which were a common means of transportation in ancient China.
  • 蒲团 - Refers to zafus, which are small cushions used for sitting in meditation or as bedding.
  • 驿卒 - Refers to postal officials who were responsible for delivering messages and documents.

Proper Nouns

  • 公 - A respectful title for a man, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 妾 - A humble term for the wife speaking to her husband.
  • 泮 - The local examination.
  • 秋闱 - The provincial examination.
  • 夜航 - Night ferries.
  • 蒲团 - Zafus (small cushions).
  • 驿卒 - Postal officials.

[211]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 后举孝廉 - Being selected as a孝廉, a type of official recommendation in the Han dynasty.
  • 捷南宫，入词林 - '捷' means to pass an exam successfully, '南宫' refers to the imperial examination hall, and '词林' is a euphemism for the imperial academy.
  • 转春坊，由卿贰，外转方伯，旋晋中丞 - A series of official promotions, with '春坊' referring to a position in the imperial court, '卿贰' to high-ranking officials, '方伯' to provincial governors, and '中丞' to a central government official.
  • 中丞官廨限于地 - Describes the limited space of the official residence.
  • 横而长，上房距鼓亭炮台密迩 - Describes the layout of the residence, with '密迩' meaning 'very close.'
  • 早晚鼓吹鸣炮 - Refers to the daily sounds of music and cannon fire.
  • 盍命稍停乎 - A rhetorical question asking if the sounds could be stopped temporarily.
  • 卿昔求之不得，今果厌烦耶 - A rhetorical statement suggesting that the official's wife had previously desired the sounds and now finds them annoying.
  • 所以日日为此者，非自鸣得意，所以补卿昔日之不足耳 - Explains that the sounds were played daily not out of self-satisfaction but to compensate for something the wife lacked in the past.
  • 从此收声，不仅稍停而已 - Means to stop the sounds completely, not just temporarily.
  • 内召为大宗伯 - Refers to being summoned to a high-ranking position, '大宗伯' being a high-ranking official in charge of rituals.
  • 妻封一品夫人 - The wife is granted the title of First-Rank Lady.
  • 遗教子孙，永不许延僧作佛事，违者以不孝论 - The official leaves a will instructing his descendants not to invite monks for Buddhist rituals, with disobedience considered filial impiety.
  • 实有憾于字画之说也 - Suggests that the official had reservations about the idea of '字画' (calligraphy and painting).
  • 芗厈曰 - '芗厈' is a person's name, possibly a scholar or commentator.
  • 许公未达时，可谓君子固穷 - Refers to the official, '许公,' and his behavior when he was not yet successful, suggesting he was a true gentleman who remained steadfast in poverty.
  • 吾乡人至今称物之不堪者，曰「许老爷铺盖」 - A colloquial expression meaning that people in the official's hometown still use the phrase '许老爷铺盖' to describe something of poor quality.
  • 竟成谚语 - The expressions have become common sayings.
  • 至其文集，为国初名家政绩，足以上动圣明 - Refers to the official's literary works, which are considered important contributions to the early Ming dynasty.
  • 不但能言之者少，即知之者亦稀 - Suggests that few people can articulate or understand the significance of the official's works.
  • 风俗之陋耶 - A rhetorical question questioning the decline in cultural refinement.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 孝廉 - A system of official recommendation in the Han dynasty.
  • 南宫 - The imperial examination hall.
  • 词林 - A euphemism for the imperial academy.
  • 春坊 - A position in the imperial court.
  • 卿贰 - High-ranking officials.
  • 方伯 - Provincial governors.
  • 中丞 - A central government official.
  • 大宗伯 - A high-ranking official in charge of rituals.
  • 一品夫人 - The title of First-Rank Lady.
  • 字画 - Calligraphy and painting.
  • 芗厈 - A person's name, possibly a scholar or commentator.
  • 许公 - The official being discussed.
  • 国初 - The early Ming dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 孝廉 - A system of official recommendation in the Han dynasty.
  • 南宫 - The imperial examination hall.
  • 词林 - A euphemism for the imperial academy.
  • 春坊 - A position in the imperial court.
  • 卿贰 - High-ranking officials.
  • 方伯 - Provincial governors.
  • 中丞 - A central government official.
  • 大宗伯 - A high-ranking official in charge of rituals.
  • 一品夫人 - The title of First-Rank Lady.
  • 字画 - Calligraphy and painting.
  • 芗厈 - A person's name, possibly a scholar or commentator.
  • 许公 - The official being discussed.
  • 国初 - The early Ming dynasty.

[212]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 巧令 - A term used to describe a clever or skilled official.
  • 攫其居停误征已免钱粮印簿 - Literally means 'seized the landlord's mistakenly collected tax exemption registration book.' Refers to a situation where someone has taken possession of a document that incorrectly lists a tax exemption.
  • 讹控者 - Refers to someone who falsely accuses or sues.
  • 状已上达，簿未呈堂 - Means 'the complaint has been submitted, but the document has not yet been presented to the court.'
  • 铁据在握 - Literally means 'holding iron evidence,' which is an idiom for having solid proof.
  • 私与往还 - Means 'to meet privately' or 'to have private dealings.'
  • 问其所欲 - Means 'to ask what he wants' or 'to inquire about his desires.'
  • 图赎其簿，销之而已 - Means 'to try to redeem the document and destroy it.'
  • 索数千金 - Means 'to demand several thousand gold coins.'
  • 倘抱赃出首 - Means 'if he were to confess and turn over the stolen goods.'
  • 中人亦连累无涯矣 - Means 'the middle person would also be implicated without limit.'
  • 皆窘于计 - Means 'all are in a difficult situation.'
  • 宪司会议时，有巧令在侧微笑 - Means 'during the conference of the constitutional officials, there was a clever official smiling.'
  • 若交职，三日办矣 - Means 'if you give me the task, I can handle it in three days.'
  • 携卷回，不动声色 - Means 'carried the case file back without showing any emotion.'
  • 上下皆曰 - Means 'everyone above and below said.'
  • 限期已到，尚未佥票 - Means 'the deadline has passed, but the ticket has not yet been issued.'
  • 得毋误乎 - Means 'is it not a mistake?'
  • 予几忘之 - Means 'I almost forgot.'
  • 升公座 - Means 'ascended the official seat.'
  • 唤三班役来前曰 - Means 'called the three ranks of officials forward and said.'
  • 善斗殴者否 - Means 'is there a good fighter?'
  • 强项者出曰 - Means 'a strong-willed person came out and said.'
  • 役能之 - Means 'the official can do it.'
  • 汝能，必知我意 - Means 'if you can, you must understand my intention.'
  • 今某处有幕客某寓 - Means 'now there is a guest in a certain place.'
  • 汝往生事激之斗 - Means 'go and provoke a fight.'
  • 但许自伤，勿许伤人 - Means 'allow only self-injury, not injury to others.'
  • 受伤即来鸣冤 - Means 'if injured, come and complain.'
  • 役曰唯 - Means 'the official replied 'yes.''

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 幕客 - Refers to a guest or a retainer, often a scholar or official in the service of a powerful person.
  • 居停 - Refers to a landlord or host.
  • 宪司 - Refers to the officials responsible for enforcing laws and regulations.
  • 三班役 - Refers to the three ranks of officials or servants.
  • 佥票 - Refers to an official document or ticket.

[213]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 即赴客寓 - Immediately went to the guest's residence. Indicates a quick action taken upon arrival.
  • 向其人大呼曰 - Shouted at him saying. Conveys a direct and forceful manner of speaking.
  • 汝从某县来，我载汝至此，今已逾月，所欠车价若干，速给我，不能再待矣 - You came from a certain county, I transported you here, it has been over a month, how much do you owe for the carriage fare? Give it to me quickly, I cannot wait any longer! Direct demand for payment.
  • 客大怒曰 - The guest became very angry and said. Indicates a strong emotional reaction.
  • 我来时仆夫非汝，何来恶棍，敢肆讹诈 - When I came, the driver was not you, what kind of rascal are you, daring to extort money? Strong accusation of extortion.
  • 役詈不已 - The official kept cursing. Continuous and harsh verbal attack.
  • 客推之出 - The guest pushed him out. Physical action of ejecting someone.
  • 役即自伤其首回 - The official immediately injured his own head and returned. Unusual and dramatic action, possibly to feign injury.
  • 县令未退堂，即呼冤入跪 - The county magistrate had not yet left the court, when the official called for a complaint and entered to kneel. Formal and urgent request for justice.
  • 正验伤 - While examining the injury. Legal procedure.
  • 以棍徒凭空讹诈具禀 - Filed a report accusing the ruffian of extorting money out of thin air. Formal accusation.
  • 役与争辩 - The official argued back. Verbal confrontation.
  • 汝既载客来，客行李若干，汝必知之 - Since you transported the guest here, how many items of the guest's luggage do you know? Question to test the official's knowledge.
  • 令逐－报明核对，役不能知，妄报数物 - The magistrate asked each item to be reported and verified, but the official could not know and falsely reported several items. Process of verification and the official's failure.
  • 客大笑，称令贤明 - The guest laughed and praised the magistrate as wise. Guest's satisfaction and the magistrate's competence.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 役 - Refers to an official or functionary, typically a lower-ranking official or bailiff.
  • 县令 - Refers to the magistrate of a county, a local government official.
  • 棍徒 - Refers to a ruffian or thug, often used to describe someone who acts aggressively or dishonestly.
  • 讹诈 - Refers to extortion or demanding money under false pretenses.
  • 退堂 - Refers to the act of leaving the court, indicating the end of a session.
  • 验伤 - Refers to the examination of an injury, typically in a legal context.
  • 具禀 - Refers to filing a formal report or complaint.
  • 争辩 - Refers to arguing or disputing a point.
  • 贤明 - Refers to wisdom and fairness, often used to praise a magistrate or judge.

Proper Nouns

  • 役 - The official or functionary.
  • 县令 - The magistrate of the county.
  • 棍徒 - The ruffian or thug.
  • 讹诈 - Extortion or false accusation.
  • 退堂 - Leaving the court.
  • 验伤 - Examination of an injury.
  • 具禀 - Filing a formal report.
  • 争辩 - Arguing or disputing.
  • 贤明 - Wisdom and fairness.

[214]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 令问之 - Polite way of asking a question, literally 'the magistrate asked him.'
  • 所报全非 - Means 'the report was entirely false.'
  • 自愿书单呈验 - Means 'willingly submitted a list for verification.'
  • 令即饬书，带役数人往客寓 - 'The magistrate immediately ordered the clerk to take several officials to the guesthouse.'
  • 当堂查验 - Means 'to inspect on the spot.'
  • 与所书单无异 - Means 'identical to the submitted list.'
  • 行箧中搜得印簿 - Means 'found a seal book in his luggage.'
  • 此系官文 - Means 'this is an official document.'
  • 何得私自携取 - Means 'how could you take it privately?'
  • 本应治罪 - Means 'should be punished according to the law.'
  • 念汝尚属斯文 - Means 'considering you are still a scholar.'
  • 姑全颜面 - Means 'to save your face.'
  • 命取火焚之 - Means 'ordered to burn it with fire.'
  • 案已上控 - Means 'the case has already been reported to a higher authority.'
  • 簿不可焚 - Means 'the book cannot be burned.'
  • 汝肯讹人，无怪人来讹汝 - Means 'if you dare to deceive others, it's no wonder others deceive you.'
  • 天道好还 - Means 'the way of heaven is to return things to their proper place.'
  • 汝知之乎 - Means 'do you know this?'
  • 第我治下，不容奸险之徒 - Means 'but in my jurisdiction, there is no room for deceitful and cunning people.'
  • 即备文递回原籍可也 - Means 'you may be sent back to your hometown with a written notice.'
  • 客知据已毁，无能为 - Means 'the guest knew the evidence was destroyed and could do nothing.'
  • 隐忍吞声而去 - Means 'silently bore the humiliation and left.'
  • 令即缴卷销案 - Means 'the magistrate immediately submitted the report and closed the case.'
  • 宪司优奖之 - Means 'the higher authority praised him highly.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 令 - Refers to a local magistrate or official.
  • 客寓 - Refers to a guesthouse or inn.
  • 行箧 - Refers to luggage or a traveling bag.
  • 印簿 - Refers to a seal book, which is an official document containing seals.
  • 斯文 - Refers to a scholar or a person of culture and learning.
  • 宪司 - Refers to a higher authority or a superior official.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence structure is somewhat elliptical, but it can be understood as the magistrate asking a question and the person responding.
  • This sentence is a complex sequence of actions performed by the magistrate and his officials.
  • The sentence is a series of statements and actions, with the magistrate explaining his reasoning and then ordering the burning of the document.
  • The guest protests, but the document is already burned.
  • The magistrate's response is a series of rhetorical questions and statements, emphasizing the consequences of deceit.
  • The guest's reaction is described in a single, elliptical sentence.
  • The magistrate's final actions are described succinctly.

[215]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 孤独少年 - Literally 'lonely young man,' used to describe a young man without family support.
  • 入赘 - Refers to the practice of a man entering his wife's family to live and work.
  • 潜投仕宦为仆 - Means 'secretly entered the service of a wealthy family as a servant,' indicating a change in social status.
  • 得主宠眷 - Means 'gained the favor of the master,' implying a positive relationship with the employer.
  • 积金四百余 - Means 'accumulated four hundred or more in silver,' indicating a significant amount of wealth.
  • 辞归 - Means 'returned home,' referring to leaving the employer's service.
  • 与其妻谋置产业 - Means 'with his wife planned to establish a business,' indicating a joint decision to start a venture.
  • 炫述于父母 - Means 'boastfully told his parents,' suggesting a boastful or exaggerated account.
  • 虐之 - Means 'abused him,' indicating mistreatment by the father-in-law.
  • 交以多金 - Means 'given a large sum of money,' referring to the transfer of wealth.
  • 恐不胜任 - Means 'fear that she cannot manage it,' indicating concern about the wife's ability.
  • 穿窬之虑 - Means 'fear of theft,' referring to concerns about security.
  • 权为收藏 - Means 'temporarily keep it for safekeeping,' suggesting a temporary arrangement.
  • 唯唯 - Means 'nodding in agreement,' indicating compliance.
  • 点交 - Means 'hand over,' referring to the transfer of the silver.
  • 八宝十六件 - Means 'eight treasures in sixteen pieces,' indicating the items transferred.
  • 酒醒而悔 - Means 'woke up from the drink and regretted,' indicating a change of mind after becoming sober.
  • 寄食我家 - Means 'living off our family,' indicating dependency.
  • 邻里咸知 - Means 'the neighbors all know,' suggesting widespread awareness.
  • 不思为汝育妻恩 - Means 'not thinking about the kindness of raising your wife,' indicating ingratitude.
  • 肆讹 - Means 'making false claims,' suggesting dishonesty.
  • 女生外向 - Means 'women are naturally inclined to be unfaithful,' indicating a sexist belief.
  • 逐其夫妇出诸大门之外 - Means 'expelled the couple from the main gate,' indicating a harsh action.
  • 冤忿兴讼 - Means 'filed a lawsuit out of resentment,' indicating legal action.
  • 以妻为证 - Means 'using his wife as a witness,' indicating the use of a spouse in legal proceedings.
  • 巧令名 - Means 'famous for cleverness,' indicating a reputation for intelligence.
  • 隔境无能为力 - Means 'cannot help from afar,' indicating a limitation due to distance.
  • 天下贤使君 - Means 'the most virtuous magistrate in the world,' indicating a high regard for a specific official.
  • 干犯义之责 - Means 'risking a breach of duty,' indicating a moral or legal obligation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 入赘 - A traditional Chinese practice where a man moves into his wife's family to live and work, often to continue the family line.
  • 仕宦 - Refers to employment in a wealthy or noble household, often as a servant or retainer.
  • 积金 - Refers to accumulating wealth, often in the form of silver or other valuable goods.
  • 产业 - Refers to a business or property, often used in the context of establishing a new life.
  • 炫述 - Refers to boasting or exaggerating, often in a social context.
  • 虐之 - Refers to mistreatment or abuse, often in a family or household context.
  • 交以多金 - Refers to the transfer of wealth, often in the context of a dowry or gift.
  • 穿窬 - Refers to theft or burglary, often in a domestic context.
  • 权为收藏 - Refers to a temporary arrangement for safekeeping, often in a trust or custodial context.
  • 唯唯 - Refers to nodding in agreement, often indicating compliance or deference.
  • 点交 - Refers to the formal transfer of property or valuables, often in a business or legal context.
  • 八宝十六件 - Refers to a specific set of valuable items, often used in the context of a dowry or gift.
  • 酒醒而悔 - Refers to a change of mind after becoming sober, often in a personal or emotional context.
  • 寄食 - Refers to living off someone else's resources, often in a dependent or parasitic context.
  • 邻里咸知 - Refers to widespread awareness or gossip, often in a community context.
  • 不思为汝育妻恩 - Refers to ingratitude, often in the context of a family relationship.
  • 肆讹 - Refers to making false claims, often in a legal or social context.
  • 女生外向 - Refers to a sexist belief that women are naturally unfaithful, often used to justify mistreatment.
  • 逐其夫妇出诸大门之外 - Refers to a harsh action, often in the context of family conflict.
  • 冤忿兴讼 - Refers to legal action taken out of resentment, often in a personal or family context.
  • 以妻为证 - Refers to using a spouse as a witness, often in a legal context.
  • 巧令名 - Refers to a reputation for intelligence or cleverness, often in a social or professional context.
  • 隔境无能为力 - Refers to a limitation due to distance, often in a legal or administrative context.
  • 天下贤使君 - Refers to a high regard for a specific official, often in a regional or local context.
  • 干犯义之责 - Refers to a moral or legal obligation, often in a professional or personal context.

Proper Nouns

  • 刘姓者 - The name of the young man, but not explicitly given.
  • 李老家 - The name of the father-in-law's family, but not explicitly given.
  • 县令 - The magistrate, but not explicitly named.

[216]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 哀之切 - Phrase meaning 'deep sorrow' or 'profound grief,' used to describe the magistrate's emotional state.
  • 令笑曰 - Phrase meaning 'the magistrate smiled and said,' introducing a statement or command.
  • 若必欲余明此讼 - Conditional phrase meaning 'if you insist that I clarify this case,' indicating a challenge or request.
  • 须暂禁囹圄 - Phrase meaning 'you must temporarily imprison him in jail,' used to propose a solution.
  • 汝愿之否 - Rhetorical question meaning 'do you agree?' or 'are you willing?' used to seek confirmation.
  • 果能明此 - Conditional phrase meaning 'if you can indeed clarify this,' indicating a condition for further action.
  • 虽刀杖加身，亦甘承受 - Phrase meaning 'even if beaten with a whip or rod, I will willingly endure it,' expressing willingness to suffer.
  • 暂禁 - Phrase meaning 'temporary imprisonment,' used to describe a short-term confinement.
  • 梏收之 - Phrase meaning 'to handcuff and detain him,' indicating the act of arresting and holding someone.
  • 移文县令曰 - Phrase meaning 'sent a letter to the county magistrate saying,' indicating official communication.
  • 日者获大盗张三 - Phrase meaning 'yesterday, we captured a notorious bandit named Zhang San,' providing context for a recent event.
  • 据供劫得某事主家银四百余两，若干锭件 - Phrase meaning 'according to his confession, he stole over 400 taels of silver and several ingots from a certain victim's home,' detailing the bandit's confession.
  • 寄顿贵县某村大窝主李老家 - Phrase meaning 'he left the stolen goods with a notorious gang leader named Li Laohu in a village in your county,' indicating where the stolen goods were hidden.
  • 希即委员带捕，查起赃银，连窝主李老解质 - Phrase meaning 'please send officials immediately to capture and investigate the stolen goods, along with the gang leader Li Laohu, to be brought to trial,' requesting immediate action.
  • 人赃并获 - Phrase meaning 'both the person and the stolen goods were captured,' indicating a successful arrest.
  • 解交此令 - Phrase meaning 'they were handed over to this magistrate,' indicating the transfer of custody.
  • 涂刘面 - Phrase meaning 'to paint the face of Liu,' indicating a form of humiliation or marking.
  • 衣以囚衣 - Phrase meaning 'to dress him in prison clothes,' indicating the act of dressing someone in prison attire.
  • 械系于堂 - Phrase meaning 'to chain him in the courtroom,' indicating the act of chaining someone in a public place.
  • 呼李老诘之曰 - Phrase meaning 'called Li Laohu and questioned him saying,' indicating the act of questioning someone.
  • 此囚供在某家劫银四百余两，八宝十六件 - Phrase meaning 'this prisoner confessed to stealing over 400 taels of silver and sixteen valuable items from a certain victim's home,' detailing the prisoner's confession.
  • 寄汝家 - Phrase meaning 'he left them with you,' indicating the act of entrusting something to someone.
  • 今所起赃数相符 - Phrase meaning 'the amount of stolen goods found matches the confession,' indicating a match between the confession and the evidence.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 囹圄 - Refers to a jail or prison, indicating a place of confinement.
  • 大盗 - Refers to a notorious bandit or criminal, indicating a serious offender.
  • 县令 - Refers to the county magistrate, a local official responsible for law and order.
  • 大窝主 - Refers to a notorious gang leader, indicating a person who leads a criminal organization.
  • 赃银 - Refers to stolen money, indicating the ill-gotten gains.
  • 八宝 - Refers to valuable items, indicating precious objects.
  • 涂刘面 - Refers to the act of painting someone's face, often used as a form of humiliation or marking.
  • 囚衣 - Refers to prison clothes, indicating the attire worn by prisoners.
  • 械系 - Refers to the act of chaining someone, indicating the act of restraining a person.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '哀之切，令笑曰：「若必欲余明此讼，须暂禁囹圄，汝愿之否？」' is a complex conditional statement that must be read as a single unit, introducing a challenge and a request.
  • The sentence '刘曰：「果能明此，虽刀杖加身，亦甘承受，况暂禁耶？」' is a response to the magistrate's challenge, expressing willingness to endure punishment.
  • The sentence '令即梏收之。乃移文县令曰：「日者获大盗张三，据供劫得某事主家银四百余两，若干锭件，寄顿贵县某村大窝主李老家，希即委员带捕，查起赃银，连窝主李老解质」云云。' is a series of actions and communications that must be read as a single sequence, detailing the magistrate's actions and the subsequent communication with the county magistrate.
  • The sentence '县令见系盗劫重情，即身自查抄，人赃并获，解交此令。乃涂刘面，衣以囚衣，械系于堂。' is a series of actions that must be read as a single sequence, detailing the county magistrate's actions and the subsequent treatment of Liu.
  • The sentence '呼李老诘之曰：「此囚供在某家劫银四百余两，八宝十六件，寄汝家，今所起赃数相符。」' is a series of actions and statements that must be read as a single unit, detailing the questioning of Li Laohu and the verification of the confession.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 汝为盗窝，罪干枭首 - 汝 means 'you,' 为盗窝 means 'being a den of thieves,' 罪干枭首 means 'guilty of decapitation,' implying a severe punishment.
  • 据实陈明，勿自庸三木也 - 据实陈明 means 'state the facts clearly,' 勿自庸三木 means 'do not be stubborn or resist the law.'
  • 此银实系小人之婿刘某寄存者 - 此银 means 'this silver,' 实系 means 'actually belongs to,' 小人之婿 means 'my humble son-in-law,' and 刘某 is the name 'Li D.'
  • 闻其得自随官，是否属实 - 闻其得自随官 means 'heard that it was obtained from an official,' 是否属实 means 'is this true.'
  • 请拘刘某与张三质之，以明小人之冤 - 请拘 means 'please arrest,' 刘某与张三 are the names 'Li D' and 'Zhang San,' 质之 means 'to confront them,' and 以明小人之冤 means 'to clear my innocence.'
  • 若见刘某，汝又将图赖矣 - 若见刘某 means 'if you see Li D,' and 图赖 means 'to fabricate a case against someone.'
  • 与其冤诛，莫若明心 - 与其冤诛 means 'rather than being wrongfully executed,' and 莫若明心 means 'it is better to be honest.'
  • 召刘某与张三质对，可见小人不知情，庶望一线生路 - 召 means 'summon,' 质对 means 'to confront,' 可见 means 'it can be seen,' 不知情 means 'innocent,' and 庶望一线生路 means 'hope for a chance of survival.'
  • 奚肯贪财舍命耶 - 奚肯 means 'would you dare,' 贪财 means 'greed for money,' and 舍命 means 'sacrifice your life.'
  • 若然，则刘某在是矣 - 若然 means 'if so,' and 则刘某在是矣 means 'then Li D is here.'
  • 乃释其桎梏，使涤面易服相见 - 乃释其桎梏 means 'then released him from his chains,' 使涤面易服 means 'had him wash his face and change clothes,' and 相见 means 'to meet.'
  • 李大惭无词 - 李大惭 means 'Li Da was ashamed,' and 无词 means 'had no words to say.'
  • 刘感激涕零而去 - 刘感激涕零 means 'Li D was deeply grateful and shed tears,' and 而去 means 'and left.'
  • 李亦从此悔过矣 - 李亦从此悔过矣 means 'Li also repented from then on.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 盗窝 - Refers to a den of thieves, a place where thieves hide and operate.
  • 枭首 - A form of execution where the head is cut off and displayed as a warning.
  • 三木 - Refers to the three wooden restraints used to bind a criminal, symbolizing resistance to the law.
  • 随官 - Refers to an official, possibly implying that the silver was obtained through official channels.
  • 翁婿 - Refers to the relationship between a father-in-law and son-in-law, a respected familial bond in Chinese culture.
  • 桎梏 - Refers to shackles or chains used to restrain criminals.
  • 涤面易服 - Refers to the act of washing one's face and changing clothes, often done to show respect or to prepare for a meeting.
  • 涕零 - Refers to shedding tears, often in a state of deep emotion.
  • 悔过 - Refers to repentance, a common theme in Chinese moral and legal discourse.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 面和而心違 - Appear harmonious on the surface but harbor resentment inwardly.
  • 潛藏而退 - Secretly hide and withdraw, indicating stealthy action.
  • 入尉手矣 - Is in the hands of the county magistrate, a colloquial expression.
  • 慎勿言 - Be careful not to speak, a warning to keep silent.
  • 若無事然 - As if nothing had happened, indicating a pretense of normalcy.
  • 入錢總簿 - Ledger or record book for financial transactions.
  • 雜稅 - Miscellaneous taxes, a broad category of levies.
  • 申明憲司 - To report to the judicial authorities, indicating a formal complaint.
  • 自請處分 - To request punishment, a gesture of self-censure.
  • 知其心而不敢言 - Knowing his heart but dare not speak, indicating fear or caution.
  • 將所藏之私簿暗毁矣 - Will secretly destroy the private ledger, indicating a clandestine act.
  • 彼诈而吾诈之 - He deceives and I deceive him in return, indicating mutual deception.
  • 强中更有强中手 - Even stronger hands exist within strength, indicating that there is always someone more capable.
  • 诪张为幻 - To deceive and create illusions, indicating cunning and deceit.
  • 不战而胜 - To win without fighting, indicating a strategic victory.
  • 欺愚而畏智 - To deceive the foolish and fear the wise, indicating a psychological dynamic.
  • 惧讼者，讼即随之 - Those who fear lawsuits will be followed by lawsuits, indicating a self-fulfilling prophecy.
  • 烛其奸又操其胜具 - To expose the deceit and hold the winning tools, indicating a strategic advantage.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 县尉 - County magistrate, a local official in ancient China.
  • 大令 - Higher-ranking official, possibly a prefect or governor.
  • 入钱簿 - Ledger or record book for financial transactions, often used by officials to keep track of income and expenses.
  • 库书 - Clerk or scribe responsible for keeping financial records.
  • 宪司 - Judicial authorities, responsible for legal matters.
  • 申明 - Act of reporting or informing formally, often to a higher authority.
  • 自请处分 - Act of requesting punishment, a gesture of self-censure or admission of guilt.
  • 私簿 - Private ledger or record book, often used to keep track of unofficial or illicit transactions.
